title: bromus squarrosus subsp. typicus
intro: Bromus squarrosus subsp. typicus, also known as rough brome or mountain brome, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and ease pain. The plant's medicinal properties make it beneficial for various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Bromus squarrosus subsp. typicus can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, allowing for a range of applications in natural remedies.
